words.meaning_en: To make something bad or unsatisfactory better
words.example_sentence: The government took steps to ameliorate the living conditions of slum dwellers.

one_word_sub.question: To make something better or improve a bad situation

words.sentence: New policies were introduced to ______ the suffering of flood victims.
words.example_sentence_hi: सरकार ने झुग्गी वासियों की स्थिति सुधारने के लिए कदम उठाए।

words.meaning_en: Hostile and aggressive; engaged in war or conflict
words.example_sentence: The belligerent nation refused to participate in peace talks.

one_word_sub.question: A person or nation engaged in war or conflict

words.sentence: The ______ attitude of the suspect alarmed the investigators.
words.example_sentence_hi: लड़ाकू राष्ट्र ने शांति वार्ता में भाग लेने से इनकार कर दिया।

words.meaning_en: A harsh, discordant mixture of sounds
words.example_sentence: The cacophony of traffic horns made it impossible to think.

one_word_sub.question: A harsh and unpleasant mixture of loud sounds

words.sentence: The street market was filled with the ______ of vendors calling out loudly.
words.example_sentence_hi: यातायात के हॉर्न की कर्कश ध्वनि ने सोचना असंभव बना दिया।

words.meaning_en: Deceitfulness; saying one thing and doing another
words.example_sentence: His duplicity was exposed when both parties compared his promises.

one_word_sub.question: The practice of deceiving by presenting a different face to different people

words.sentence: The diplomat's ______ led to the breakdown of negotiations.
words.example_sentence_hi: उसकी छल-प्रवृत्ति तब उजागर हुई जब दोनों पक्षों ने उसके वादों की तुलना की।

words.meaning_en: Lasting for a very short time
words.example_sentence: Fame can be ephemeral—here today, gone tomorrow.

one_word_sub.question: Something that lasts for only a very short period

words.sentence: The joy of victory was ______, fading within hours of the match.
words.example_sentence_hi: प्रसिद्धि क्षणभंगुर हो सकती है—आज है, कल नहीं।

words.meaning_en: Based on a mistaken belief; logically unsound
words.example_sentence: His fallacious argument was quickly dismantled in the debate.

one_word_sub.question: An argument that is logically wrong or misleading

words.sentence: The advertisement made a ______ claim about the product's benefits.
words.example_sentence_hi: उसका भ्रामक तर्क बहस में जल्दी ही खंडित हो गया।

words.meaning_en: Fond of company; sociable
words.example_sentence: A gregarious person makes friends wherever they go.

one_word_sub.question: A person who enjoys the company of others and is very sociable

words.sentence: Her ______ nature made her a natural leader in group activities.
words.example_sentence_hi: एक मिलनसार व्यक्ति जहाँ भी जाता है दोस्त बना लेता है।

words.meaning_en: A person or thing that announces or signals the approach of another
words.example_sentence: Dark clouds are often a harbinger of rain.

one_word_sub.question: Something that indicates the approach of a future event

words.sentence: The cuckoo's call is considered a ______ of spring.
words.example_sentence_hi: काले बादल अक्सर बारिश के अग्रदूत होते हैं।

words.meaning_en: A person who attacks or criticises cherished beliefs or institutions
words.example_sentence: The young scientist was an iconoclast who challenged outdated theories.

one_word_sub.question: A person who challenges and opposes traditional beliefs or practices

words.sentence: As an ______, she refused to follow the established norms of her profession.
words.example_sentence_hi: युवा वैज्ञानिक एक परंपरा-विरोधी था जिसने पुरानी सिद्धांतों को चुनौती दी।

words.meaning_en: To throw or drop from an aircraft or ship; to abandon or discard
words.example_sentence: The company decided to jettison its failing division.

one_word_sub.question: To throw away or get rid of something no longer needed

words.sentence: The pilot had to ______ excess fuel before making an emergency landing.
words.example_sentence_hi: कंपनी ने अपने असफल प्रभाग को त्यागने का फैसला किया।

words.meaning_en: Using very few words; brief and concise
words.example_sentence: His laconic reply — just 'No' — surprised everyone.

one_word_sub.question: A person who uses very few words to express themselves

words.sentence: The general was known for his ______ orders that left no room for confusion.
words.example_sentence_hi: उनका संक्षिप्त उत्तर — केवल 'नहीं' — ने सभी को चौंका दिया।

words.meaning_en: Very generous or forgiving, especially towards a rival or someone less powerful
words.example_sentence: The magnanimous winner congratulated his opponent warmly.

one_word_sub.question: A person who is very generous and forgiving, especially to rivals

words.sentence: It was ______ of him to forgive those who had wronged him.
words.example_sentence_hi: उदार विजेता ने अपने प्रतिद्वंद्वी को गर्मजोशी से बधाई दी।

words.meaning_en: Wicked, criminal, or extremely immoral
words.example_sentence: The nefarious scheme was uncovered by the investigators.

one_word_sub.question: An extremely wicked or criminal act or plan

words.sentence: The gang was involved in ______ activities that harmed the community.
words.example_sentence_hi: जाँचकर्ताओं ने उस दुष्टतापूर्ण योजना को उजागर किया।

words.meaning_en: To make unclear or confusing; to bewilder
words.example_sentence: Politicians often obfuscate facts with technical jargon.

one_word_sub.question: To deliberately make something unclear or confusing

words.sentence: The report was designed to ______ rather than inform the public.
words.example_sentence_hi: राजनेता अक्सर तकनीकी शब्दजाल से तथ्यों को अस्पष्ट कर देते हैं।

words.meaning_en: Extreme poverty; destitution
words.example_sentence: Despite his penury, he managed to send his children to school.

one_word_sub.question: A state of extreme and utter poverty

words.sentence: The old poet died in ______, his talents unrecognised during his lifetime.
words.example_sentence_hi: अत्यंत गरीबी के बावजूद, वह अपने बच्चों को स्कूल भेजने में सफल रहा।

words.meaning_en: Complaining in a petulant or whining manner
words.example_sentence: The querulous customer complained about every minor issue.

one_word_sub.question: A person who constantly complains or whines about minor things

words.sentence: His ______ tone made it difficult to have a productive conversation.
words.example_sentence_hi: शिकायती ग्राहक ने हर छोटी बात पर शिकायत की।

words.meaning_en: Having an obstinately uncooperative attitude
words.example_sentence: The recalcitrant employee refused to follow the new policy.

one_word_sub.question: A person who stubbornly refuses to obey rules or authority

words.sentence: The ______ student was sent to the principal's office for the third time.
words.example_sentence_hi: अड़ियल कर्मचारी ने नई नीति का पालन करने से इनकार कर दिया।

words.meaning_en: A person who acts obsequiously towards someone important in order to gain advantage
words.example_sentence: The boss was surrounded by sycophants who never told him the truth.

one_word_sub.question: A person who flatters powerful people to gain personal advantage

words.sentence: A true friend is more valuable than a thousand ______s.
words.example_sentence_hi: मालिक उन चापलूसों से घिरा था जो उसे कभी सच नहीं बताते थे।

words.meaning_en: Eager or quick to argue or fight; aggressively defiant
words.example_sentence: His truculent behaviour made negotiation impossible.

one_word_sub.question: A person who is aggressively defiant and eager to fight or argue

words.sentence: The ______ prisoner refused to cooperate with the prison authorities.
words.example_sentence_hi: उसके उग्र व्यवहार ने बातचीत को असंभव बना दिया।

words.meaning_en: To waver between different opinions or actions; to be indecisive
words.example_sentence: She vacillated between accepting the job offer and staying in her hometown.

one_word_sub.question: To keep changing one's mind or opinion; to be unable to decide

words.sentence: Leaders who ______ in times of crisis lose the confidence of their followers.
words.example_sentence_hi: वह नौकरी का प्रस्ताव स्वीकार करने और अपने गृहनगर में रहने के बीच डगमगाती रही।

words.meaning_en: Deliberate and unprovoked; showing reckless disregard
words.example_sentence: The wanton destruction of public property is a criminal offence.

one_word_sub.question: An act of deliberate cruelty or destruction without reason

words.sentence: The ______ cruelty shown to animals shocked the entire neighbourhood.
words.example_sentence_hi: सार्वजनिक संपत्ति का जानबूझकर नुकसान एक आपराधिक अपराध है।

words.meaning_en: Dislike or prejudice against people from other countries
words.example_sentence: Xenophobia increases during times of economic hardship.

one_word_sub.question: An intense fear or dislike of people from foreign countries

words.sentence: The policy was widely criticised as an example of institutional ______.
words.example_sentence_hi: आर्थिक कठिनाई के समय विदेशी-द्वेष बढ़ जाता है।

words.meaning_en: The defining spirit or mood of a particular period of history
words.example_sentence: Social media perfectly captures the zeitgeist of the 21st century.

one_word_sub.question: The general mood or spirit that characterises a particular historical period

words.sentence: The film captured the ______ of the 1990s with remarkable accuracy.
words.example_sentence_hi: सोशल मीडिया 21वीं सदी की युग-भावना को पूरी तरह दर्शाता है।

idioms.example_sentence: She was burning the midnight oil to finish her project before the deadline.

idioms.hindi: कठिन परिस्थिति को साहस से सहना
idioms.meaning_en: To endure a painful or difficult situation with courage
idioms.example_sentence: He decided to bite the bullet and confront his boss about the issue.

idioms.meaning_en: Something that seems bad at first but turns out to be good
idioms.example_sentence: Losing that job was a blessing in disguise — it led him to a better career.

idioms.meaning_en: Pursuing a mistaken or misguided course of action
idioms.example_sentence: If you think he's the culprit, you're barking up the wrong tree.

idioms.idiom: hit the nail on the head
idioms.meaning_en: To describe exactly what is causing a situation or problem
idioms.example_sentence: You hit the nail on the head when you said the team lacked motivation.
